name: GlitchTip Error Tracking API
what_it_does: Open-source Sentry-compatible error tracking platform with REST API for managing issues, alerts, and performance monitoring. Self-hostable alternative to Sentry with Sentry SDK compatibility.
use_cases: Tracking application errors and exceptions programmatically with Sentry-compatible SDKs
Querying error issues and counts for automated triage and alerting agents
Managing error alert rules and notification channels via API
Building custom error dashboards using GlitchTip's REST API for engineering teams
Self-hosting error tracking with full data sovereignty and no per-event costs
not_for: Organizations needing enterprise features like AI-powered root cause analysis
Teams that rely heavily on Sentry's performance monitoring features
Production use cases requiring commercial SLA and support
best_when: An agent manages error tracking in a privacy-sensitive or budget-constrained environment where Sentry's pricing is prohibitive and self-hosting is acceptable.
avoid_when: You need Sentry's full feature set including advanced performance monitoring, cron monitoring, or AI features.

auth.notes: User auth tokens from GlitchTip settings. Sentry-compatible token format. Tokens scoped to read/write per resource type. DSN keys for SDK event ingestion are separate from API tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted: completely free, unlimited events; GlitchTip.com cloud: free tier with limits
pricing.paid_tiers: GlitchTip.com hosted: from $20/month; Enterprise self-hosted support available
pricing.notes: MIT open-source — fully free to self-host. GlitchTip.com offers managed hosting. Dramatically cheaper than Sentry for high-event-volume applications.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no rate limiting; cloud plan-dependent

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Sentry-compatible API means Sentry documentation partially applies. REST API documented. Error responses are JSON. Sentry SDKs work with DSN pointed at GlitchTip instance.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Issue grouping is deterministic — same error produces same issue fingerprint. Alert rule updates are idempotent by rule ID.
known_agent_gotchas: API compatibility with Sentry is not 100% — some Sentry endpoints may be missing
Self-hosted requires PostgreSQL, Redis, and Celery setup — operational overhead
Event grouping heuristics may differ from Sentry — same errors may group differently
GlitchTip cloud requires pointing Sentry SDKs to glitchtip.com DSN endpoint

security_score_components.security_notes: MIT open-source — fully auditable. Django/Python backend with established security practices. Self-hosted option provides full data control. TLS configuration required for self-hosted production deployments.
